quick_facts.label: Award and status
quick_facts.value: B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture is listed by ICAR as a four-year bachelor’s programme in Agricultural Sciences and as a professional degree for the 2026–27 ICAR admission cycle.

quick_facts.label: Typical length
quick_facts.value: The programme is normally four years across eight semesters; an official Anand Agricultural University B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture offering follows this structure.

quick_facts.label: ICAR all-India admission route
quick_facts.value: For 2026–27, ICAR uses CUET (ICAR-UG) for its all-India bachelor-degree admission process in Agriculture and Allied Sciences. It covers 20% of participating agricultural-university seats, with 100% coverage at certain named ICAR/central institutions; it is not the sole admission route for every B.Sc. Agriculture seat in India.

quick_facts.label: CUET (ICAR-UG) subject baseline
quick_facts.value: For the 2026 ICAR route, candidates must have passed Class 12 with at least three core subjects from Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology, and Agriculture/Inter-Agriculture, while the final subject combination and other conditions can differ by admitting university.

quick_facts.label: Practical learning
quick_facts.value: A representative agricultural-university curriculum includes laboratory and field-oriented courses and a final-year Student READY/RAWE-style placement, village/KVK attachment, agro-industry attachment, and experiential-learning components; exact credits and modules vary by university.

quick_facts.label: Choosing a college
quick_facts.value: ICAR publishes updated lists of accredited agricultural universities, colleges, and programmes. Applicants should verify both institutional/programme accreditation and the university’s own admission rules before accepting an offer.

sections.heading: Eligibility: Class 12 science/agriculture preparation is central
paragraphs: For admission through CUET (ICAR-UG) 2026, ICAR requires a recognised 10+2/equivalent qualification and at least three Class 12 core subjects selected from Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology, and Agriculture/Inter-Agriculture. Candidates must take CUET subjects consistent with their Class 12 subjects; a mismatch can make them ineligible for ICAR counselling.

The ICAR 2026 bulletin sets a minimum aggregate of 50% for General, OBC-NCL, UPS, and EWS candidates and 40% for SC, ST, Third Gender, and PwBD candidates for this ICAR route. It also sets a minimum age of 16 years as of August 31, 2026. These are ICAR-route rules, not a substitute for programme-specific eligibility set by an individual university.

University-level conditions matter. ICAR expressly notes that institutions may impose additional subject combinations, age limits, or other statutory conditions. For example, an agricultural university may use a state entrance examination for its state-seat admissions rather than the ICAR all-India route.
bullets: Before applying, compare your Class 12 subject combination with the target programme’s published requirement.
Treat ICAR/CUET eligibility as one admissions channel; check the target university’s prospectus for state quota, domicile, category, age, language, and counselling requirements.
Verify the exact programme title on the offer letter: B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture is distinct from horticulture, forestry, agribusiness management, and other allied degrees.

sections.heading: Duration and academic format
paragraphs: ICAR’s 2026–27 undergraduate admission bulletin places B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture among 13 bachelor’s programmes of four-year duration. A typical delivery model is eight semesters, as illustrated by Anand Agricultural University’s four-year, eight-semester residential programme.

The final year is often heavily experiential rather than purely classroom-based. In AAU’s published structure, Semester VII is Student READY for 20 weeks and Semester VIII is an Experiential Learning Programme for 24 weeks. The names, sequence, and credit weights are university-specific, so applicants should use the curriculum issued by the college that will award their degree.
bullets: Expect practical, field, village-extension, research-station, and agro-industry exposure in addition to lectures and laboratories.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: The curriculum is interdisciplinary: it combines crop and soil sciences with biological sciences, farm engineering, economics, extension, and management. In AAU’s published B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture curriculum, early study includes soil science, agricultural microbiology, agro-meteorology and climate change, horticulture, plant pathology, agricultural informatics, mathematics, agronomy, genetics, and agricultural economics.

Later study broadens into crop-production technology, entomology and integrated pest management, agricultural finance and cooperation, farm machinery, agricultural extension, plant breeding, agricultural marketing, seed technology, sustainable agriculture, geoinformatics and precision farming, biotechnology, organic farming, watershed management, entrepreneurship, post-harvest management, and value addition.

This is a representative official curriculum rather than a nationally identical semester-by-semester prescription. Students should compare the current syllabus of each shortlisted university, especially the amount of fieldwork, elective choice, Student READY/RAWE, internship, and experiential-learning provision.
bullets: Crop, soil, plant-health, genetics, horticulture, and livestock foundations.
Farm systems, water/soil conservation, machinery, climate, and precision-agriculture exposure.
Agricultural economics, finance, marketing, extension, rural sociology, communication, and entrepreneurship.
Applied training through village/KVK, research-station, and agro-industry attachments in programmes that include Student READY/RAWE or equivalent.

sections.heading: Admission routes: CUET (ICAR-UG) and university/state processes
paragraphs: For the 2026–27 ICAR all-India quota process, the relevant examination is CUET (ICAR-UG). ICAR states that eligible candidates register and fill choices for online counselling; allocation of a programme, seat, and agricultural university follows merit rank, choices, and fulfilment of eligibility conditions. ICAR also says that direct nomination is not available for admission through its route without qualifying CUET (ICAR-UG).

The phrase “ICAR AIEEA” remains useful historical shorthand for ICAR entrance admissions, but the current undergraduate bulletin titles the route CUET (ICAR-UG). ICAR’s AIEEA-PG nomenclature continues for postgraduate admission. Applicants should therefore follow the current year’s official notices rather than rely on older AIEEA-UG instructions.

Not every B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture seat is allotted through ICAR counselling. State agricultural universities may separately use state-level entrance tests and merit formulas for their own admissions. AAU’s published example uses GUJCET plus Higher Secondary marks for its common Gujarat agricultural-university procedure. Check the target university’s prospectus and counselling portal for its active route, dates, fee, documents, and seat category.
bullets: For ICAR all-India seats: follow the current CUET (ICAR-UG) bulletin, take the permitted CUET subject combination, and participate in ICAR online counselling if eligible.
For state/university seats: check the university’s own admissions notice; entrance tests and merit rules can differ materially.
Do not assume a CUET score alone guarantees admission: eligibility is checked by the admitting university and programme-specific conditions apply.

sections.heading: After graduation: higher study, public recruitment, agribusiness, and entrepreneurship
paragraphs: For subject specialisation and research, graduates can pursue M.Sc. programmes in Agriculture and allied disciplines. ICAR’s 2026–27 notice identifies AIEEA-PG as the all-India examination route for postgraduate degree programmes in Agriculture and Allied Sciences; the applicable annual information bulletin governs discipline eligibility, seats, scholarships, and counselling.

For agribusiness management, a four-year B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture can meet the academic-background requirement for programmes such as MANAGE’s PGDM (Agri-Business Management). Its 2026 policy accepts agriculture-science/agriculture-related bachelor’s degrees of at least four years, subject to minimum marks and a valid CAT score. Comparable MBA/PGDM programmes have their own selection rules.

For entrepreneurship, NABARD’s Agri-Clinic and Agri-Business Centres framework lists agriculture graduates from SAUs, CAUs, and ICAR/UGC-recognised universities among eligible beneficiaries. The scheme describes ventures such as custom hiring of farm equipment, input and advisory services, post-harvest management, and market-linkage services; finance, training, and subsidy availability must be confirmed under the live scheme rules.

For public-sector examinations, a B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture is an eligible bachelor’s degree background for the Indian Forest Service because UPSC specifically accepts a bachelor’s degree in Agriculture. Civil Services requires a recognised graduate degree; eligibility alone does not replace the annual age, attempt, nationality, examination, and application conditions. Agriculture is also an accepted four-year degree for IBPS Agricultural Field Officer (Scale I) in the CRP SPL-XVI notification. NABARD Grade A is a recruitment process whose advertised disciplines, vacancies, age limits, and degree criteria vary by notification, so candidates should use the current NABARD advertisement rather than assume a permanent agriculture-specific vacancy.
bullets: M.Sc. Agriculture and allied specialisations through university processes and, for ICAR’s all-India route, AIEEA-PG.
MBA/PGDM in Agri-Business Management, with institution-specific entrance-test, marks, and interview requirements.
Agri-entrepreneurship and advisory/service enterprises, including possible ACABC support where the current scheme rules are met.
Competitive recruitment pathways: UPSC Civil Services, UPSC Indian Forest Service, IBPS Agricultural Field Officer, and notification-dependent NABARD Grade A opportunities.

Private-sector roles may include agri-inputs, seed, crop-protection, farm advisory, procurement, food/value-chain, plantation, sales, and operations roles; the employer’s job description determines whether a bachelor’s degree alone is sufficient.
